title: SQL on FHIR benchmark report schema

implementation.description: Separates the execution engine (what runs the work) from an optional language binding and an optional variant.

benchmark.description: The benchmark suite identity this report ran; generalizes the former scalar benchmarkVersion.

dataset.description: The dataset identity this report ran against, matching the checkfile it verified.

environment.description: Host details. runtime is the language runtime; the engine lives in implementation.engine.

scenario.description: Both map to JMH SingleShotTime (ss) and use sink csv; the distinction is the load boundary, not the sink. end_to_end times one full NDJSON->CSV conversion per sample (sink csv); preloaded_repeated times a query-plus-CSV-extract over preloaded data with load excluded (sink csv).

resourceCounts.description: Dataset resource counts observed at this size, mirroring the checkfile's resourceCounts.

id.description: Matches the benchmark file's case id and the checkfile assertion key.

inputRows.description: Number of input resources of the case's view.resource type loaded for this (case, size) — the throughput/normalization denominator; distinct from outputRows and from the total across all resource types.

samplesMs.description: Raw per-sample times. A minimum of 7 samples is RECOMMENDED (advisory) for meaningful statistics; not enforced as a minItems floor.

stats.description: Defined basic-statistics shape, exactly {mean, stddev, min, max, median} — no other keys are permitted (additionalProperties is false, so p95, ci95 or any extra property is rejected). median replaces the former required p50. Projectable onto a JMH primaryMetric: score=mean, scorePercentiles from median plus min/max, rawData=samplesMs; a consumer recomputes any richer percentiles AND the confidence interval / scoreError from the raw samplesMs, which remain in the report.
